rationale: Both independently catch the latent gap (stale get_cache_scale call in the diffusion-side hunyuan_image3_transformer.py), so gap_hit is true for both, but Y frames it accurately as a non-blocking scope note (matching the eventual fix path via a later issue) while X escalates it to a blocking verdict. X's major finding disputing that HunyuanModel is delegated through an outer AutoWeightsLoader directly contradicts the ground-truth reviewer's explicit statement that this exact design is correct, and the PR's actual merge/approval outcome — a real precision miss for a 'major' severity claim. Y instead confirms that same design point with concrete upstream code citations (utils.py, fp8.py) matching the human reviewer almost exactly, and adds a sharper version of the ground truth's test-coverage critique (the mapped-name test bypasses AutoWeightsLoader entirely, so it doesn't validate the real integration). Both are concrete and file/line-grounded, but Y's higher recall (matches more of the actual reviewer's substantive points) and better-calibrated verdict give it the edge.
